name: The Incubator
description:
So... I was laying in my bed sick, and I noticed that my room was a little bit hotter than the rest of the house and this was born. Enjoy. You are any magical creature, you are a baby. Here is a description of the layers.

the cave can be separated into layers.

1. the surface or 'ice' layer:

this is the highest up layer and the most unstable. 'restless stages' can happen with gaps of 3-6 days at this level making it the most unpredictable. this is where you might find creatures like small lizards, moth people, or small ice dragons. it is not made of ice despite the name, it is simply the coldest.

2. the warm or sunny layer:

this is above the middle but below the surface. its 'restless stages' can happen with gaps from 1-4 weeks. this is considered the best layer as it is home to most easy to train small dragons, elves, common serpents and basilisks, small hybrids, small griffins, and almost all merfolk. it is also not too hot for humans. it is one of the most populated layers and is recommended for beginners. it is also where the second most heat producing plants are.

3. the middle or ember layer:

this is the middle layer. it is where all the trainable rare creatures are. creatures like centaurs, medium dragons, basilisks and serpents, large griffins, medium hybrids, and most elves. it's 'restless stages' can happen with gaps from 1-12 months long. only confident explorers dare to enter this layer. it is where the most heat producing plants are.

4. the smoldering layer:

this is under the medium level and the last layer that is cool enough to support humans. it's restless stages can happen with gaps from 1-10 years long. only highly trained and experienced thieves step foot in this layer.

5. the flame layer:

this is the first layer that is too hot for humans. large dragons, some basilisks, certain hybrids, and some of the most feared monsters can be found here. it's 'restless stages' can happen with gaps from 1-7 centuries long.

6. the death layer:

this layer is home to unspeakable horrors and it's said that even dragon scouts have never returned from it. nobody knows what lives there. it is also extraordinarily hot. its 'restless stages' can happen with gaps from 1-10 centuries long.

7. the lava layer:

this layer is estimated to be as hot as lava. it is home to massive, powerful dragons and griffins as well as a few serpents and elves. its 'restless stages' can happen with gaps from 20-100 centuries long.

8. the dragon den:

this layer is home to the most ferocious dragons. its 'restless stages' can happen with gaps from 1-5 millenias long.

9. the magma layer:

the layer right above the core of the earth. it is home to creatures said to be gifted with 'god-like' abilities. its 'restless stages' can happen with gaps from 1-2 trillion years long.

10. t
creator: Meowzercat
